name: Crostata Di Fragole
recipeInstructions:
1. Use a sharp knife to cut the crostata to keep the berries from toppling and lessening the elegant symmetry of the dessert.
2. FOR THE PASTA FROLLA: Combine the dry ingredients in a bowl and mix well. Rub in the butter until it is absorbed, ensuring the mixture remains cool and powdery without becoming pasty. Beat the egg and stir in with a fork until the dough holds together; knead briefly to form a smooth disk. Wrap in plastic and refrigerate at least 1 hour, or until firm.
3. FOR THE STRAWBERRIES: Rinse and hull berries. Arrange the best ones in a 9-inch circle on wax paper around the tart. Slice remaining berries, add sugar and lemon juice, then toss to combine and cover with plastic wrap.
4. Preheat oven to 350°F and set a rack in the middle level. Roll three-quarters of the dough into a 9-inch disk, transfer to a lined cookie sheet, and trim to a 9-inch diameter. Pierce the dough all over with a fork at 1/2-inch intervals.
5. Combine the scraps with the remaining dough, roll into a thin cylinder about 30 inches long, moisten the edge of the base, arrange the cylinder on it, and press to adhere. Mark diagonal lines with a knife.
6. Bake for about 25 minutes until golden and firm. Cool the tart shell on a rack, place on a platter, spread evenly with jam, arrange whole strawberries at the edge, drain sliced berries, pile in the center, and dust with sugar.
